title: James Webb Space Telescope Captures the Cigar Galaxy’s Brilliant Stellar Halo in Pristine Detail
gist: NASA released new James Webb Space Telescope images of Messier 82, the Cigar Galaxy, revealing its stellar halo in detail and hinting at the galaxy's turbulent past; the piece also recounts the origin of the Messier catalogue by 18th-century comet hunter Charles Messier.

insights.claim: NASA released new James Webb Space Telescope images of Messier Object 82, the Cigar Galaxy, that capture its brilliant stellar halo in pristine detail and offer hints into the galaxy's turbulent past.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: JWST continues delivering frontier imaging; relevant to the space-instrumentation and astronomy ecosystem.
insights.quote: NASA just released new images of Messier Object 82, the Cigar Galaxy, captured by the James Webb Space Telescope
insights.locator: Messier’s designations are still used today

insights.claim: The Messier catalogue of 100+ objects originated as 18th-century French astronomer Charles Messier's list of 'Not Comets' that distracted from his comet hunt; his designations remain in use today.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Historical context on enduring astronomical nomenclature.
insights.quote: he compiled a list of more than 100 of them, known as the Messier catalogue
insights.locator: So to keep track of the eye-catching Not Comets
